input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule F.1.13 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: f.1.13
            TITLE: rollover protection envelope

            CONTENT:
            rule F.1.13: Rollover Protection Envelope
F.1.13 Rollover Protection Envelope 
The Primary Structure plus a plane from the top of the Main Hoop to the top of the Front 
Hoop, plus a plane from the top of the Main Hoop to the rearmost Triangulated structural 
tube, or monocoque equivalent.   
* If there are no Triangulated Structural members aft of the Main Hoop, the Rollover 
Protection Envelope ends at the rear plane of the Main Hoop

final_answer: rule F.1.13: Rollover Protection Envelope F.1.13 Rollover Protection Envelope The Primary Structure plus a plane from the top of the Main Hoop to the top of the Front Hoop, plus a plane from the top of the Main Hoop to the rearmost Triangulated structural tube, or monocoque equivalent. * If there are no Triangulated Structural members aft of the Main Hoop, the Rollover Protection Envelope ends at the rear plane of the Main Hoop
